title: Boston Research Data Center
gist: This is an NBER program-description page for the Boston Census Research Data Center, not a research paper. It describes how qualified researchers access non-public federal microdata; there are no investable findings, only institutional/process detail.

insights.claim: The Boston Census Research Data Center, a US Census Bureau-NBER partnership open since 1994, gives qualified researchers access to non-public microdata from the Census Bureau, NCHS, AHRQ, BLS, and BEA.
insights.speaker: NBER
insights.so_what: Context only: explains the data infrastructure behind many NBER microdata papers; no direct market signal.
insights.quote: the BRDC provides qualified researchers the opportunity to perform statistical analysis on non-public microdata from the Census Bureau
